commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh For the seller shall not return to that which is sold, although they were yet alive: for the vision concerns the whole multitude, which shall not return; neither shall any strengthen himself in the iniquity of his life. Sold, as in the year of jubilee, Leviticus xxv. (Calmet) Now there shall be no recovery. (Worthington) They shall be driven from the land. By supplying the former negation, (Calmet) we may render "Nor", (Haydock) which seems better. (Calmet) It may also mean, "those who are now alive "shall witness these things. (Haydock) Back. It is not a mere threat.
